title: Create hello_world Python module returning Hello, world! string
job:
## When the spec is ambiguous — ask instead of guessing

If the supporting documents above do not make a particular design
choice clear, DO NOT guess. Your build will be rejected and you will
waste a review cycle.

Instead:

1. Create a draft pull request titled **exactly** `NEEDS_CLARIFICATION: <your question>` (one sentence, imperative).
2. The PR body should include: the specific file/function you were working on, what options you see, and which one you'd default to if forced to choose.
3. Commit no code changes unless they are a minimal scaffold required to open the PR.
4. Do not merge the PR. Mission Control will detect the title, stop this job, and surface your question to the user. Once answered, the updated PRD will come back with a fresh dispatch and you'll start over with the clarified spec.

Use this for genuine ambiguity only — missing a provider name, an
unspecified enum, contradictory requirements. Do not use it for
mundane implementation choices (variable names, import order, etc.) —
pick a reasonable default for those.

---

## Your task

Create a new Python source file at the project root exposing a `hello_world()` function that returns the string 'Hello, world!'. The module must be importable from the project root so it can be invoked manually. Keep the implementation minimal — a single function definition with no side effects at import time.
